steps.title: Fastest path: mark the angle information first
steps.content: Mark the given angle relationships first: equal angles, supplementary angles, parallel-line angles, and triangle sums of $180^\circ$. Most of these questions become a short angle chase once the givens are organized.

steps.title: Step 1
steps.content: List the known angles. Triangle $ABC$: $\angle A = 35^\circ$, $\angle B = 85^\circ$. Triangle $XYZ$: $\angle X = 35^\circ$, $\angle Y = 85^\circ$. We already have two pairs of equal corresponding angles: $\angle A = \angle X$ and $\angle B = \angle Y$.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.content: The AA (Angle-Angle) Similarity Theorem states: if two angles of one triangle equal two angles of another triangle, the triangles are similar. Two pairs of equal angles are sufficient - no third angle is needed.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.content: Confirm the third angles are implicitly equal anyway. Angle sum: $\angle C = 180^\circ - 35^\circ - 85^\circ = 60^\circ$ and $\angle Z = 180^\circ - 35^\circ - 85^\circ = 60^\circ$. They are forced equal by the first two pairs.
